117065.explanation: Aldermen and the mayor must be selected based on quality.

117066.explanation: This is the role of the municipal council; with a referendum, we see too great a risk that only opponents will respond.

117069.explanation: We would like as many people as possible to enjoy our environment, which may include a campsite. Whether this is always appropriate, we cannot assert in advance. However, the initial attitude could be positive, as with a shop.

117072.explanation: There is insufficient information yet to form an opinion. It is important to carefully weigh everything before deciding.

117073.explanation: Employers have a responsibility here, but it is also true that not all responsibility lies with them.

117077.explanation: Assist with permits when private individuals want to organize this.

117081.explanation: This definitely needs to become easier.

117082.explanation: Sports create connection and lower healthcare costs. The investment in a sports membership is less than the costs we prevent by doing so.

117083.explanation: Reporting, including by a local broadcaster, is very important. Whether this currently requires extra funding, we do not expect. We also do not support budget cuts.

117091.explanation: There are no signals that this is necessary, but as soon as there are, action must be taken.

117094.explanation: This helps combat the housing shortage and allows flow in the housing market. So yes, but under agreed conditions.

117097.explanation: Each case is unique and deserves its own assessment.

117098.explanation: Costs are rising, so it is logical that this limit increases accordingly.

117101.explanation: This sounds nice, but all roads would then have to be adjusted, which will be an unaffordable exercise. Each case should be assessed separately and where possible designated as a 30 km/h road.

117104.explanation: If this is possible, then yes please.

117109.explanation: Yes, people with refugee status fleeing, for example, war, must be helped as well as possible. This should also be proportional to the size of the municipality.

117112.explanation: No extra money is currently available. The swimming pool is part of the sports company Kaag and Braassem, which plays an important social role in the community (all sports centers in the municipality are included). Children learn to swim, exercise, and have a lot of fun there.

117116.explanation: High means different things to different people. Apartment complexes may be taller in Roelofarendsveen than, for example, on Kaag. Six to seven floors in Roelofarendsveen is high enough to contribute to the housing shortage without excessively impacting the village character.

117117.explanation: Provided this is done under established conditions and requirements, for example, to prevent exploitation.

117118.explanation: Our municipality consists of 11 unique villages, each with its own facilities. When the village has a (small) shopping area, it is nice if it is designed with greenery and hospitality.

117119.explanation: Parking needs will change in the coming years, and we must respond to that. Lower parking standards mean it is easier to build and create more public green space.

117122.explanation: Wind turbines are allowed but must cause as little nuisance as possible and should not be too tall.

117123.explanation: If an association has a need, they can approach the municipality. Each case will be judged individually.

117065.explanation: Diversity in the college of mayor and aldermen adds value. It is about putting the right people in the right places (qualities).

117066.explanation: The largest referendum is held every four years during municipal elections. We have principles and a good network with our residents and supporters.

117069.explanation: Where possible, we must provide space for this; but it must fit within the living environment.

117071.explanation: Does not fit our policy and environment.

117072.explanation: As long as the distribution law is in effect, we must comply with the law.

117073.explanation: The pressure on the housing market is high; this concerns seasonal housing and is therefore temporary. Accommodation must be appropriate in the living environment and cause no nuisance or inconvenience.

117077.explanation: The new law has just come into force. We will engage in dialogue with our residents about what is needed.

117081.explanation: Not on the land; but yes on the yard/building plot.

117082.explanation: We want to encourage a healthy lifestyle among our residents in every possible way.

117083.explanation: Only if necessary (!). Our broadcaster fulfills a need; local news is important.

117087.explanation: We have only a few municipal monuments.

117089.explanation: Safety at home and outside is important; if it appears that more effort is needed, we will support this.

117090.explanation: We have not received any signals that this is necessary in our villages.

117091.explanation: We are too small a municipality for such an institution; our residents can go to places like Leiden.

117094.explanation: Temporary permanent living in a holiday park, we understand that. Permanent living, for an indefinite period, these homes are not suitable for that and often the park itself isn’t either.

117097.explanation: We have a clear income limit for residents who do or do not qualify; this has been democratically established and decided.

117098.explanation: The minimum wage has increased extra in recent years and with it the income threshold to qualify for assistance.

117101.explanation: Traffic safety for all road users (!). Our villages are not really suitable for high speeds.

117104.explanation: We have been increasing our property tax (OZB) for years only by indexation; that is realistic and well explainable.

117109.explanation: This is the law, only 8% of social rental homes go to status holders.

117112.explanation: We live in a very water-rich municipality; swimming is a basic need. We must continue to properly support the outdoor pool De Kleine Oase in Leimuiden.

117114.explanation: A coffeeshop quickly has a attracting effect and causes nuisance for the neighborhood. We want to prevent (drug) nuisance on the street.

117116.explanation: Provided it is appropriate/spatially well substantiated.

117117.explanation: This causes (parking) nuisance for the neighborhood. We are in favor of, among other things, splitting houses, flexible housing on private grounds (under conditions), raising existing buildings.

117118.explanation: If by village center a 'community center' is meant, we agree with this statement. Coming together and meeting is important.

117119.explanation: We have our parking standard; it is known that this sometimes is tight. If opportunities arise, we should certainly seize them; however, not as an end in itself.

117122.explanation: We voted against the Local Energy Strategy (LES) because the frameworks for wind turbines (health, noise, distance standards, height) were lacking. If wind turbines have to come, they must fit into the environment and the total impact must be clear.

117123.explanation: The associations contribute greatly to the vitality in the villages. We have the MAG-agenda (Social Agenda) for this; by implementing the goals described in it, they receive extra funding.

117065.explanation: Equal distribution is preferred but we consider quality more important.

117066.explanation: We have a lot of contact with our residents but these signals are sometimes insufficient to make a council change course. For drastic plans, a referendum is important.

117069.explanation: Recreation and sale of regional products must be possible at the farmer; Samen (Together) is in favor of this.

117071.explanation: We are against large-scale distribution centers by entrepreneurs from outside the region. Distribution centers, preferably small-scale, for local entrepreneurs we find acceptable.

117072.explanation: We support the distribution law and must accommodate people who really need asylum, integrate them well into our community, with our norms and values, and offer them good opportunities.

117073.explanation: If that is possible in a good way, then gladly. In other cases, there are alternative accommodations.

117077.explanation: We are in favor of a fireworks show, provided it is affordable and we can find cooperation with enthusiastic fireworks enthusiasts to do this TOGETHER (in the middle of the Braassem).

117081.explanation: We have the environmental vision for this and for rural areas there are logical and clear rules.

117082.explanation: This is (partly) already done for people with a low income via the sports fund.

117083.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives subsidy from the national Journalism Stimulus Fund.

117089.explanation: Currently the number of enforcement officers (boa’s) is adequate; through active enforcement this should be sufficient.

117090.explanation: Only at unsafe cycle paths and at identifiable problem locations such as the currently relevant Sportpad.

117091.explanation: If that is necessary, then we are in favor. There are no indications for that yet.

117094.explanation: In times of housing shortage, this seems like a logical choice. Recreation must remain possible, and therefore this cannot apply to all dwellings.

117097.explanation: The consequences of this lead to problems and additional costs for the municipality itself.

117101.explanation: We have many cyclists and walkers in our villages. Moreover, the villages are too small to drive through quickly.

117104.explanation: We are not the most expensive municipality.

117109.explanation: Housing these people is a legal task. We support equal treatment for all housing seekers.

117112.explanation: Not for the time being, as long as the sports path has not yet been delivered.

117114.explanation: We do not see any positive effects coming from coffeeshops.

117116.explanation: High residential towers do not fit in the village. A few taller buildings where appropriate are possible.

117117.explanation: There is also a need for that.

117118.explanation: If there is a need, but nothing is mandatory.

117119.explanation: Investments by the municipality in parking lots are seen by us as a solution for parking nuisance on main roads.

117122.explanation: High wind turbines do not fit into our landscape.

117123.explanation: The subsidy granting is working well now.

117065.explanation: D66 wants a diverse council, but a strict 50/50 requirement can exclude the best candidate. We want to choose based on quality and open selection, actively seeking talented women and men. That way Kaag en Braassem gets the best council.

117066.explanation: D66 wants to involve residents in governance, not just occasionally. This means we should continuously engage in dialogue with residents. A referendum can fit within this. It is a tool that can be used in exceptional cases with the right agreements.

117069.explanation: D66 wants to promote small-scale recreation. This keeps our municipality lively and strengthens the local economy. Therefore, we embrace such initiatives by farmers, with clear agreements about traffic, nature, and neighbors.

117071.explanation: D66 wants the development of business parks for our local entrepreneurs so they have the space. We oppose large national distribution centers.

117072.explanation: D66 believes the municipality must comply with the national task assignment. We implement the dispersion law. We stand for humane reception and remain committed to the plan to meet our obligations together with Nieuwkoop. Whether the asylum seekers' center will be located in our municipality is still unknown.

117073.explanation: D66 sees that seasonal workers are needed in our municipality; their housing must also be properly arranged. D66 believes employers should take responsibility and provide decent, safe housing on their own grounds, with clear rules. This prevents nuisance and protects employees.

117077.explanation: D66 believes the New Year's Eve celebration should be enjoyed. We favor a centrally organized (fireworks) show per village or a large show on the Braassem. We also want to explore fireworks clubs where residents can responsibly/certified organize a local show themselves.

117081.explanation: D66 wants to give farmers space to develop. Converting the farm/nursery can contribute to tackling the housing shortage. For the hinterland and open meadows, D66 supports stricter rules. These areas cannot just be fully built up.

117082.explanation: D66 considers exercise important and participation should not be a luxury. Being a member of a sports club is good for your health and can also be nice for social contacts. In our municipality, this possibility already exists with youth sports funds and adult funds. We want to continue this.

117083.explanation: D66 finds it important that local news is easily accessible. But in terms of support, the municipality should not go beyond the legally required assistance (Media Act).

117087.explanation: Kaag en Braassem has no municipal monument care. We cannot cut back on this.

117089.explanation: Safety starts with visibility on the streets. D66 finds it good that capacity was recently increased. This leads to faster action against nuisance. We do see that village safety is under pressure, so we believe more enforcement capacity is still needed.

117090.explanation: D66 wants real safety, not false security. We prefer police and enforcement time to be spent on street presence and talking to people rather than watching cameras. Temporary camera surveillance could be an option if there is a real problem.

117091.explanation: No one should feel unsafe at home. D66 finds it important that there is an easily accessible, safe shelter for those fleeing domestic violence. This can be arranged regionally, also for residents of Kaag en Braassem.

117094.explanation: D66 thinks it is important to adhere to the current agreements regarding enforcement of vacation homes.

117097.explanation: Making ends meet is already difficult for those with low incomes. D66 wants to retain remission: this prevents debts and stress and keeps help available for those who truly need it. Fair and humane policy.

117098.explanation: Many people just fall outside support schemes. The cause of poverty is not always on the income side but often on the expenditure side. Poverty sometimes even occurs in the 'middle group'. D66 prefers targeted support with customized solutions and quick debt assistance instead of stretching the eligibility limits.

117101.explanation: D66 supports 30 km/h speed limits in the villages. You want to arrive home safely; this especially applies to children and cyclists. Fewer accidents, less noise, and less cut-through traffic. On real through routes, 50 km/h can remain with safe design.

117104.explanation: Yes, who wouldn’t want taxes to go down? Everyone does. But if you choose to lower the property tax (OZB), it comes at the expense of important services. Therefore, we choose for the lowest possible tax while maintaining the level of services.

117109.explanation: D66 supports good, humane housing for status holders. The municipality has the legal task to house status holders, provided they meet the established requirements. This can be in social rental housing. Other housing forms can also be developed.

117112.explanation: ‘De Tweesprong’ and ‘De Kleine Oase’ are facilities that D66 does not want to lose in the municipality. For sports, exercise and recreation but also to give swimming lessons so children grow up safely in our water-rich municipality.

117114.explanation: Coffeeshops have not been allowed in our municipality for years. There have been no requests to change this. D66 therefore sees no reason to change the policy. A coffeeshop does not fit the health objectives of D66 for our municipality.

117116.explanation: More homes are urgently needed; building upwards is very logical then. But 'high residential towers' sound very unattractive. D66 chooses village-style building, multi-story apartments (up to 6 floors) in appropriate places (for example in Braassemerland).

117117.explanation: Shared housing can ease the tight market; at the same time there are concerns about nuisance. D66 supports room rental with clear rules and supervision. This way we help starters and students without burdening neighborhoods.

117118.explanation: D66 wants all villages to have their own community and meeting place. This does not always have to be in the form of a neighborhood center or village hall. Often this is already present in villages.

117119.explanation: D66 sees that there is high parking pressure in many neighborhoods. Still, we do not choose to sacrifice green spaces in neighborhoods for parking. When redesigning, we look for possibilities to create space without sacrificing greenery.

117122.explanation: D66 opposes the large plans of the province. However, D66 wants the municipality to contribute to the efforts outlined in the Regional Energy Strategy. D66 wants to take its own responsibility and place a limited number of wind turbines - specifically 1 or 2.

117123.explanation: D66 considers associations extremely important for the villages in Kaag & Braassem. They deserve good support. This does not always have to be financial but can include help with permit applications, recruiting volunteers, or organizing administrative cooperation.

117065.explanation: Quality and suitability come first. Parties determine their own candidates. Enforcing equality does not fit with a liberal governance culture.

117066.explanation: Residents deserve good participation, but the municipal council is democratically elected to make balanced decisions and bear responsibility.

117069.explanation: Side activities such as shops or small-scale recreation strengthen farmers’ income models and keep the rural area economically vital.

117071.explanation: Business space in our municipality is scarce and we prefer to preserve it for our local entrepreneurs. Distribution centers require a regional or national approach/vision.

117072.explanation: We prioritize the safety of residents and entrepreneurs in the reception of asylum seekers. As long as national legislation applies, we will continue looking for a suitable location in the rural area. We involve local residents from the start and ensure clear agreements about security and supervision.

117073.explanation: Where possible, entrepreneurs should be able to house their employees themselves. This prevents extra pressure on the housing market and also has practical advantages.

117077.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Initiatives should come from society, possibly with limited support.

117081.explanation: Housing development requires coordination. Without clear frameworks, fragmentation occurs, and housing construction in the rural area often requires provincial approval.

117082.explanation: There are already regional and national sports programs for people with low incomes. These work well. It is not necessary for the municipality to add extra local policies or resources.

117083.explanation: An additional contribution is possible if it strengthens independent local journalism and has a clearly added value.

117087.explanation: There is no municipal subsidy for characteristic buildings. Owners can use national schemes via the National Restoration Fund.

117089.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and compliance. This requires sufficient capacity on the streets.

117090.explanation: Kaag en Braassem is not a major urban municipality. Camera surveillance does not fit here as a standard tool.

117091.explanation: Protection is essential, but this requires a regional approach. Not every facility needs to be organized locally.

117094.explanation: Holiday parks are intended for recreation. Only for a limited number of non-vital parks can conversion to permanent residence be considered through tailored solutions, under strict conditions and with remittance of the value increase.

117097.explanation: The focus should be on work, guidance, and preventing debts. Structural debt forgiveness is not a sustainable solution.

117098.explanation: The current income limits align with national rules. Expansion leads to high additional costs without the aid becoming more targeted.

117101.explanation: 30 km/h zones can contribute to safety, provided roads are or become suitable for this and it is applied logically.

117104.explanation: The property tax rate (OZB) can be lowered. The municipality must remain strict on its spending and avoid unnecessarily burdening residents.

117109.explanation: Priority for status holders is unfair to other home seekers. Everyone should have equal opportunities for social rental housing. Status holders can be offered temporary housing with flexible homes to take a first step out of the reception center.

117112.explanation: Much money has already been invested and there are reserves for future adjustments, such as a slide. Additional structural support is not necessary.

117114.explanation: We are not fundamentally against a coffee shop, provided it operates under strict conditions and with attention to safety and livability.

117116.explanation: High-rise buildings (five stories or higher) are possible if they fit in the environment. This is possible in a few locations in Roelofarendsveen.

117117.explanation: Converting houses into multiple units can help with the housing shortage if well regulated and without nuisance to the neighborhood.

117118.explanation: Preserve what exists. Where possible, we stimulate ownership by residents themselves through associations or foundations.

117119.explanation: Parking pressure is high. Additional parking spaces are needed because residents and entrepreneurs are heavily dependent on cars.

117122.explanation: No wind turbines in the Green Heart. Protection of the open polder landscape and a safe distance from homes make this unsuitable.

117123.explanation: Associations should be able to function largely independently. Therefore, we do not burden them with property tax (which has been abolished), but see no need for structural additional subsidies on top of the existing Social Agenda (MAG) funds.

117065.explanation: The best alderman in the right place.

117072.explanation: If the request comes from the government, we can look at small-scale possibilities.

117073.explanation: We support opportunities to create housing on private land where the rules for employee and employer are clear and fair.

117077.explanation: We support this if it is organized in a central location and all residents can enjoy it.

117081.explanation: To reduce the housing shortage, it is desirable to allow more room here.

117082.explanation: Only for residents under 18 years old.

117089.explanation: Safety is an important theme for us. In the new council term, it must be determined how efforts will be divided. It is currently unclear whether the current effort is sufficient.

117090.explanation: Only in certain areas where more nuisance and crime are experienced.

117094.explanation: Provided the house and grounds are suitable for this.

117098.explanation: This must be considered on an individual basis.

117101.explanation: Speed can only be reduced where necessary.

117116.explanation: We support apartments with a maximum of 5 floors.

117117.explanation: We support the possibility of splitting homes.

117119.explanation: We support small-scale, central parking garages with 2 or 3 floors.

statements.title: Kaag en Braassem should be allowed to have tall wind turbines.

titleParts.text: In Kaag en Braassem mogen hoge

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The residents of Kaag en Braassem should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Kaag en Braassem moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Kaag en Braassem should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.text: In Kaag en Braassem moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Kaag en Braassem should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.text: In Kaag en Braassem moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: High residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Roelofarendsveen.
Kaag en Braassem should stop subsidizing Zwembad De Tweesprong.
Kaag en Braassem should have a women's shelter.

titleParts.text: In Kaag en Braassem moet een

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should give more money to local associations.
